name: Tuvalu
avoid: November-April is wetter, hotter, and more disruption-prone for inter-island boats, so outer-island plans can slide by a day or more.
Do not try to force Nanumea and Vaitupu into a very short trip; Tuvalu works best with buffer nights because boats are infrequent and weather dependent.
Cash is essential across Tuvalu, and guesthouses, meal stops, and boat tickets are easiest to manage if you arrive from Fiji with enough Australian dollars for the full stay.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, stay on Funafuti and add only one outer island. Trying to cover both Nanumea and Vaitupu on a short visit usually wastes too much time waiting on boats.

cities.name: Funafuti
cities.notes: Funafuti is Tuvalu's only realistic arrival base, built around the Fongafale airstrip, the conservation-area lagoon day trip, simple guesthouses, and one of the world's least-visited national capitals.

cities.name: Nanumea
cities.notes: Nanumea is the far-north atoll extension for community-hosted stays, quiet lagoon swims, and a much more local version of Tuvalu once the supply boat has dropped you off.

cities.name: Vaitupu
cities.notes: Vaitupu slows the trip down even further with broad lagoon views, village walks around Asau and Faleapoi, and boat-linked reef or sandbank outings when sea conditions co-operate.

itinerary.sim: Tuvalu Telecom service is strongest on Funafuti and much weaker once you head to the outer islands, so buy a prepaid SIM in Fongafale and download offline maps plus boat contacts before leaving the capital.

Funafuti.name: Funafuti
Funafuti.note: Funafuti deserves at least two proper days: one for the conservation-area boat and one for the airstrip, handicrafts, and easy island pacing before you gamble on any outer-island sailing.

days.theme: Runway Arrival, Vaiaku Orientation and a Classic Tuvalu Sunset

activities.name: Funafuti airstrip and WWII runway walk on Fongafale
activities.tip: The old wartime runway is still the island airstrip, and outside flight windows it doubles as Funafuti's social promenade for walks, volleyball, and sunset people-watching.

activities.name: Tuvalu Women's Handicraft Centre browsing
activities.tip: Browsing is free, but woven fans, shell jewelry, and baskets usually make this the easiest place to support local makers with a small cash purchase.

activities.name: Sue's Kitchen lunch stop
Bicycle hire for the Fongafale lagoon-side road
activities.tip: Simple day rentals usually land around AUD 5-15 depending on whether your lodge arranges the bike and how long you keep it.

days.theme: Funafuti Conservation Area Motu Day and Clear-Water Snorkeling

activities.name: Funafuti Conservation Area visitor permit
activities.tip: Foreign visitor permits are commonly quoted around AUD 30, and the dry May-October window usually gives the calmest water and best visibility.

activities.name: Guesthouse-arranged Funafuti Conservation Area boat and snorkel outing
activities.tip: Most shared day boats with mask, fins, and guide time price around AUD 50-80 per person when enough travelers split fuel.

activities.name: Outer motu picnic or lagoon swim
activities.tip: Bring your own drinks and snacks or ask the lodge to pack lunch because there are no restaurants once the boat leaves Fongafale.

days.theme: Lagoon Snorkel Repeat, Village Errands and Boat-Departure Buffer Time

activities.name: Guesthouse boat to a Funafuti lagoon snorkel spot outside the conservation area
activities.tip: Private or small-group outer-motu boats often cost about AUD 60-100 total, so the per-person price drops if you can share with other guests.

activities.name: Snorkel gear hire if not included
activities.tip: Basic mask-and-fin rental is often around AUD 5-10 a day if your guesthouse does not already lend gear.

activities.name: Motorbike rental for quick Fongafale errands
activities.tip: Motorbike hire is usually around AUD 10-25 per day and is the easiest way to check boat offices, guesthouses, and the northern swimming end of the island.

Nanumea.name: Nanumea
Nanumea.note: Treat Nanumea as a simple outer-island immersion rather than a checklist stop: one arrival day and one extra lagoon-and-village day is usually enough before supplies or weather shift again.

days.theme: Passenger Boat North, Community Check-In and Lagoon Time

activities.name: Funafuti to Nanumea inter-island passenger boat
activities.tip: Government passenger-boat fares to Tuvalu's outer islands are commonly quoted around AUD 50-75 one way, but the bigger issue is irregular timing rather than price.

activities.name: Nanumea lagoon swim and shoreline walk
Nanumea community guesthouse or homestay contribution
activities.tip: Outer-island stays are usually arranged through local hosts or the island council and commonly land near AUD 20-40 a night including very simple meals.

days.theme: Village Paths, Church Stop and a Slow Boat-Life Day

activities.name: Nanumea village and church walk
activities.tip: A small donation is appreciated if a host opens community spaces or church grounds for visitors, especially on quieter non-Sunday afternoons.

activities.name: Host-arranged small-boat reef edge or fishing outing
activities.tip: Expect the price to hinge on fuel and who shares the boat; on Nanumea this is usually an informal cash arrangement through your host rather than a formal tour desk.

activities.name: Walking and cycling between settlements on the atoll

Vaitupu.name: Vaitupu
Vaitupu.note: Vaitupu is best kept flexible: arrive when the boat works, walk the main villages, and spend one extra day on the lagoon rather than trying to over-plan a place with very light tourism infrastructure.

days.theme: Inter-Island Arrival, Asau Village and Broad Lagoon Views

activities.name: Nanumea or Funafuti to Vaitupu passenger boat sector
activities.tip: If you are not coming directly from Funafuti, confirm whether the ship is calling at Vaitupu on your sailing because outer-island routing can change with cargo and weather.

activities.name: Asau village walk and waterfront pause
Vaitupu guesthouse or family homestay contribution
activities.tip: Like Nanumea, Vaitupu accommodation is generally simple community lodging booked through local contacts rather than branded hotels.

days.theme: Vaitupu Lagoon, Faleapoi Village and a Reef-Edge Boat Outing

activities.name: Vaitupu lagoon boat and snorkel session
activities.tip: Local lagoon outings are normally arranged informally with a host family or fisherman, and the dry season gives the best chance of calmer reef conditions.

activities.name: Faleapoi village weaving or handicraft visit
activities.tip: Budget a host-family contribution if someone demonstrates weaving, coconut preparation, or other everyday skills.

activities.name: Walking or cycling between Vaitupu villages

connections.method: Most guesthouses are within a short walk or very quick pickup from the runway area on Fongafale.
Take the government passenger boat north; departures are infrequent, basic, and often weather delayed, so keep at least one buffer night on Funafuti.
Continue only if the same inter-island vessel or the next scheduled ship is calling southbound via Vaitupu; otherwise return to Funafuti first.
Return by passenger ship and keep your final Fiji flight on a later day, not the same day, because outer-island sailing times move around.

extras: Tuvalu is one of the world's least-visited countries, so the real planning skill here is patience: confirm boats repeatedly and treat extra waiting time as part of the trip.
Outside Funafuti, you should assume patchy power, limited fresh produce, and almost no formal tourism services, so carry snacks, refillable water, and cash in small notes.
Sunday is especially quiet across Tuvalu, with church activities taking priority and very little transport or commerce moving beyond essentials.
Reef shoes, sunscreen, a dry bag, and offline confirmations matter more than fancy gear because nearly every worthwhile day here includes boat spray, coral edges, or long waits in the heat.
